title_en: Violence is reshaping British political life
title_zh: 暴力正在重塑英国政治生活
rubric_en: The shocking murder of Ann Widdecombe will only reinforce the trend
rubric_zh: 安·威德科姆遇害的震惊事件只会强化这一趋势

paragraphs.en: IT is often said after an atrocity that terrorists will not prevail. Those who practise politics by peaceful means, the mantra goes, will triumph over those who choose violence. If only it were true. The bleaker reality is that, in Britain, the threat of violence against parliamentarians is having the effects that at least some of its perpetrators desire: driving people from public life and shrinking the sphere of debate.
paragraphs.zh: 每当暴行发生后，人们常说恐怖分子不会得逞。那套常见说法是，通过和平方式从事政治的人，终将战胜选择暴力的人。要是真的如此就好了。更阴暗的现实是，在英国，针对议员的暴力威胁正在产生至少部分施害者想要的效果：把人赶出公共生活，并压缩辩论的空间。

paragraphs.en: More than a quarter of candidates at the last general election in 2024 said they had considered not standing because of concerns about harassment and intimidation, according to a survey of candidates by the parliamentary authorities. Some 58% said they had been physically attacked or touched against their will; 77% had suffered intimidating acts such as damage to property or being followed in the street. A quarter said it led them to hesitate before making decisions; more than two in five said they avoid getting involved in some policy debates altogether.
paragraphs.zh: 据议会当局对候选人进行的一项调查，在2024年上一次大选中，超过四分之一的候选人表示，由于担心骚扰和恐吓，他们曾考虑不参选。约58%的人说自己曾遭到人身攻击或被违背意愿地触碰；77%的人遭遇过恐吓行为，例如财产受损或在街上被跟踪。四分之一的人说，这导致他们在作出决定前会犹豫；超过五分之二的人说，他们干脆避免参与某些政策辩论。

paragraphs.en: Those figures will surely rise. On July 9th Ann Widdecombe, a 78-year-old former Tory MP who became a member of the populist-right Reform UK, was found dead in her remote bungalow in Devon. Police later concluded that she had been murdered in a “targeted attack”. A 28-year-old white British man from Rotherham, 430km away, has been arrested on suspicion of terrorism offences. The motive is unclear.
paragraphs.zh: 这些数字几乎肯定会上升。7月9日，78岁的前保守党议员安·威德科姆被发现死在她位于德文郡偏僻地区的平房里。她后来加入了民粹右翼政党英国改革党。警方随后认定，她是在一次“有针对性的袭击”中被谋杀的。一名来自430公里外罗瑟勒姆的28岁英国白人男子，因涉嫌恐怖主义罪行被捕。作案动机尚不清楚。

paragraphs.en: Ms Widdecombe was the third prominent politician in Britain to be murdered in little over a decade. Jo Cox, a Labour MP, was killed in the street by a far-right extremist in 2016. David Amess, a Conservative lawmaker, was stabbed at a constituency surgery in 2021 by a radical Islamist.
paragraphs.zh: 威德科姆是十多年里英国第三位遭谋杀的知名政治人物。2016年，工党议员乔·考克斯在街头被一名极右翼极端分子杀害。2021年，保守党议员戴维·埃默斯在一次选区接待选民活动中被一名激进伊斯兰主义者刺死。

paragraphs.en: The phenomenon is not new. From the 1970s to the 1990s, Irish republicans targeted mostly senior Conservatives, killing four MPs and narrowly missing the entire cabinet. Yet such acts at least were governed by a degree of organisation and strategy that gave the security services a chance to predict and disrupt them.
paragraphs.zh: 这种现象并不新鲜。从20世纪70年代到90年代，爱尔兰共和派主要把资深保守党人作为目标，杀害了四名议员，并险些杀死整个内阁。然而，这类行动至少还受到一定组织和战略的约束，使安全部门有机会预测并破坏它们。

paragraphs.en: Today’s threat is diffuse and unpredictable, the work of individuals rather than groups. Ms Widdecombe seems an improbable target: a Conservative minister in the 1990s, she left Parliament in 2010. When not on “Strictly Come Dancing”, a reality-TV show, or giving interviews about her garden, she spoke on immigration and justice for Reform. Amess’s killer had researched over 250 MPs before attacking the mild-mannered backbencher. Cox had been in Parliament little over a year before she was killed. If they could be targets, so could almost anyone in public life.
paragraphs.zh: 如今的威胁则分散且难以预测，更多是个人所为，而非团体行动。威德科姆似乎是个不太可能的目标：她曾在20世纪90年代担任保守党大臣，2010年离开议会。除了参加真人秀节目《舞动奇迹》，或接受关于她花园的采访外，她还会代表改革党就移民和司法问题发声。杀害埃默斯的人在袭击这位性情温和的后座议员前，曾研究过250多名议员。考克斯遇害时，进入议会才一年多。如果他们都可能成为目标，那么公共生活中的几乎任何人也都可能成为目标。

paragraphs.en: MPs feel under siege. It is impossible to tell the difference between unpleasant cranks and potential murderers. Penny Mordaunt, a former Tory minister, said this week that she had received more than 700 threats to kill or rape her; there hadn’t been a day for four years without a live police investigation or court case into her pursuers.
paragraphs.zh: 议员们感到自己处于围困之中。令人讨厌的怪人和潜在杀人犯之间根本无法区分。保守党前大臣彭妮·莫当特本周表示，她收到过700多次威胁，要杀死她或强奸她；过去四年里，没有一天没有针对跟踪骚扰她的人进行中的警方调查或法庭案件。

paragraphs.en: Protecting MPs has become a significant operation. The task is split between the Parliamentary Security Department and the Metropolitan Police’s Parliamentary Liaison and Investigation Team, which investigates crimes against lawmakers and co-ordinates with provincial police forces. A specialist Security Information and Risk Analysis Service weighs intelligence. MPs’ homes and constituency offices are routinely fitted with arson-proof letter boxes and panic rooms.
paragraphs.zh: 保护议员已经成为一项庞大的行动。这项任务由议会安全部和伦敦警察厅的议会联络与调查小组分担，后者负责调查针对议员的犯罪，并与地方警察部队协调。一个专门的安全信息与风险分析服务机构负责评估情报。议员的住所和选区办公室通常会安装防纵火信箱和安全屋。

paragraphs.en: The question of what protection MPs are entitled to has become bitterly partisan. On July 14th a man was arrested for threatening on social media to shoot Nigel Farage, Reform’s leader. “I am going to shoot you in the head if you win,” he wrote. Reform accuses the police and government of purposefully denying its leaders adequate security for political reasons, a charge the authorities deny. It has an interest in having such a row: the party claimed that a previously undeclared £5m ($6.7m) donation to Mr Farage, now subject to intense scrutiny, was for the purpose of private security arrangements.
paragraphs.zh: 议员有权获得何种保护，这个问题已经变得党派对立尖锐。7月14日，一名男子因在社交媒体上威胁要枪击改革党领袖奈杰尔·法拉奇而被捕。他写道：“如果你赢了，我就朝你头部开枪。”改革党指责警方和政府出于政治原因，故意不给其领导人提供足够的安保；当局否认这一指控。改革党有意挑起这样的争吵：该党声称，此前未申报的一笔给法拉奇的500万英镑（670万美元）捐款，是用于私人安保安排的，而这笔捐款如今正受到严密审查。

paragraphs.en: It is often said, after an attack, that violence will not change a country’s way of life. But the fear of violence is now woven deeply into the fabric of British politics. ■
paragraphs.zh: 袭击发生后，人们常说，暴力不会改变一个国家的生活方式。但如今，对暴力的恐惧已经深深织入英国政治的肌理之中。■

paragraphs.en: For more expert analysis of the biggest stories in Britain, sign up to Blighty, our weekly subscriber-only newsletter.
paragraphs.zh: 如需阅读更多关于英国重大新闻的专家分析，请订阅Blighty，我们每周一期、仅面向订阅者的新闻简报。
